entry.title: homepage
entry.content:
About me

Hanyi Wu is an interdisciplinary data scientist and geographer interested in health risk and inequality in the changing environment, crop mapping, timeseries satellite data reconstruction, and soil erosion. He developed remote sensing approaches combined with machine learning in order to characterize forms of cities not only at large scales but also a spatially-explicit resolution close to people’s living environment to understand health.
This is a simple page for my academic website. Thank you for your attention !

Education

2022-2025Beijing Normal UniversityMScCartography and GIS
2018-2022
Nanjing University of Information Science & Technology
BSc
Remote sensing




Award and honors

2022
National Scholarship (Undergraduate Student)
2022
Outstanding Student of Jiangsu Province (Undergraduate Student)




Research interests

Environmental health risk and inequality

heat health risk assesment, human exposure, environmental justice

Agricultural remote sensing

crop mapping (especially early and in-season large-scale mapping), timeseries satellite data reconstruction in agricultural scenarios, phenology extraction

Soil erosion

large-scale soil erosion mapping in Google Earth Engine

Fundings

2020
Jiangsu Province College Students' Platform for Innovation and Entrepreneurship Training Program (No. 202010300038Z)




Recent update
entry.summary: About me
